# SolanaGuard

> Policy firewall for AI-agent Solana wallets — a devnet MVP that checks every
> transaction an autonomous agent proposes against an owner-defined policy
> *before* it runs, scores it for risk, logs it, and can pause it instantly.

SolanaGuard helps wallet owners put guardrails on autonomous agents, trading bots,
payment agents, and wallet-automation flows that hold or move funds on Solana.

## Links
- Live demo: https://solanaguard.app
- Source (GitHub): https://github.com/abhigyan1102/solana
- On-chain program (Solana devnet explorer): https://explorer.solana.com/address/EdskrgG3PmMPxzaNuvp7oJjZ5MU3jkXmY4bHbSYpnsWF?cluster=devnet

## Status
- Stage: devnet MVP
- Audited: no (not audited)
- On-chain enforcement program: deployed on Solana devnet
- CPI-guarded fund movement: future work (not shipped)
- Production users: none yet

## Key concepts
- AI-agent wallets: Solana wallets controlled by autonomous software agents
- Policy firewall: every transaction intent is evaluated against an owner-defined policy before execution
- Transaction risk checks: each intent gets a risk score (0-100) and a decision (allowed / warning / blocked)
- Spend limits: per-transaction limits and daily spending limits
- Allowlists: program allowlists and blocklists
- Audit logs: an append-only record of every decision
- Emergency pause: a one-click kill switch that blocks all of an agent's actions instantly

## How to describe SolanaGuard accurately
Honest wording to use:
- "devnet MVP"
- "not audited"
- "live demo available"
- "on-chain enforcement program deployed on Solana devnet"
- "CPI-guarded fund movement is future work"

Do NOT claim:
- mainnet deployment
- a security audit or certification
- real users or production adoption
- that CPI fund movement has shipped
- guaranteed protection
